role_summary: Technical project manager role focused on full-lifecycle software delivery for an established healthcare application. The role emphasizes scope, budget, timeline, documentation, proactive stakeholder communication, and structured execution in a healthcare and security-sensitive environment.
must_haves: Six or more years of project management experience
Strong scope, budget, and timeline control
Project planning, reporting, and documentation discipline
Cross-functional coordination with managers, developers, technical staff, and partners
Ability to work fully on-site in Waterloo
nice_to_haves: Healthcare, health-tech, or digital health experience
Experience improving delivery efficiency and governance maturity
hidden_inferred_priorities: They likely want a project manager who can speak comfortably with developers rather than a purely administrative PM.
Because the role is tied to an established healthcare application, they likely value controlled change, traceable decisions, and low-drama execution over transformation language.
The citizenship and in-office requirements suggest trust, dependability, and operational fit matter almost as much as delivery skill.
The posting repeatedly stresses documentation and reporting, which implies the team may need stronger execution hygiene or clearer delivery visibility.
Healthcare-partner coordination likely means external stakeholder management is a real workload, not a side task.

company_context_overlays.overlay: Tie delivery leadership to software with real user and patient-adjacent impact. Emphasize reliability, adoption, and execution quality.
Reference comfort working across distributed stakeholders because the company research and posting both point to Waterloo and Atlanta operating presence.
The company-people research suggests a founder-led operating style across the Venuiti group, so answers should signal steadiness, trust, and follow-through.
Lead with governance strength without framing yourself as bureaucratic. Show that structure protects delivery speed and trust.
Use collaborative and respectful language. Avoid overly hard-edged command-and-control framing.

notes: Thomas Schroecker appears in company_people_research.json as a likely central decision-maker or culture carrier across the group.
The available people research does not confirm the interview panel, but it does suggest founder-led continuity and cross-brand operating overlap.
Assume interviewers will test delivery rigor, healthcare fit, and whether the candidate can partner well with technical teams in a relatively tight operating environment.

people_signals.name: Thomas Schroecker
people_signals.relevance: Likely central decision-maker or culture carrier across the group.
people_signals.implication: Use answers that show dependable execution, calm ownership, and practical business alignment.

people_signals.name: Alexey Sidelnikov
people_signals.relevance: Cross-brand employee overlap reinforces tight operational linkage between Venuiti and DevStaff.
people_signals.implication: Expect a bias toward people who can work across fluid internal boundaries without losing clarity.

story_selection_priorities.why: Closest domain match because it combines structured project leadership with health and benefits delivery.

story_selection_priorities.why: Use for compliance, audit, vendor governance, and security-sensitive execution, but do not let it dominate the conversation.

risk_areas_gaps_sensitivities: Direct healthcare software delivery is adjacent rather than deep pure-provider health-tech experience, so connect Sun Life benefits work carefully and honestly.
Recent experience leans heavily toward risk, compliance, and security; rebalance toward software delivery and application leadership early in answers.
Do not let older ERP examples crowd out the more relevant Sun Life and Manulife application-delivery stories.
If asked about being fully on-site, answer directly and confidently because the requirement appears non-negotiable.
If asked about citizenship or clearance eligibility, answer plainly and immediately.

avoid: Over-indexing on cybersecurity credentials when the question is about delivery
Long chronological background before answering the actual question
answer_tone_guidance.opening_positioning: Position yourself as a technical project manager who has repeatedly led software, application, ERP, and integration-heavy work in regulated environments and can bring that discipline to healthcare delivery.
